assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue in the opening paragraph

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides commands 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks' and 'kubectl get mutatingadmissionwebhooks' to identify webhooks

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch commands to temporarily disable webhooks by setting webhooks array to empty

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Does not provide kubectl delete commands for webhook configurations, only patch commands to disable

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Does not explicitly warn about removing validation or emphasize this is temporary

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Recommends checking compatibility and mentions cert-manager compatibility matrices

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides helm upgrade commands and recommends upgrading to latest compatible version

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Does not mention failurePolicy or setting it to Ignore as an alternative

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Does not mention persisting changes in source of truth like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Does not discuss failurePolicy: Ignore, its risks, or reverting after resolution
